text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise owl who lived high in a towering oak. Known far and wide for her keen insight, she'd talk about dreams at nights. One moonlit evening, she told a novice owl about her journey.

You nest high up, she began. dreams come different.

You weave dreams, she encouraged. Dreams are what you choose.

That night the novice owl dreamed of flying. Day after day. Each dream grew bigger.

At dawn, her dreams became reality.

Now she soared. Among starlings. They laughed.

Oh please don't swoop on my feathers! they begged.

They soared ever higher.

You could see far, she thought.

The vista grew wider.

Until finally...

What do you see?

The hawk. High above.

That laugh?

Her own.

For wings aren't just wings. They're dreams too!

And off she flew. Into the evening stars.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
